intro: Caroxylon pulvinatum, also known as the false saltbush, is a shrub-like succulent plant species native to the arid regions of southern Africa. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, and its health benefits include treating various ailments such as wounds, skin conditions, and fever, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Caroxylon pulvinatum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and topical creams, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including skin conditions and wounds.
